subject: UX & Research
title: Breadcrumbs and Location Awareness
description: A 60-second lesson on Breadcrumbs and Location Awareness.

hook.text: Home > Electronics > Phones > iPhone 15. That trail of links tells you exactly where you are. That's a breadcrumb.

buildup.text: Named after Hansel and Gretel, breadcrumbs show the path from the homepage to the current page.

discovery.text: Breadcrumbs reduce lost users by 25%. They let users jump back to any level without hitting the back button.

twist.text: On mobile, breadcrumbs take precious space. Many mobile sites drop them. But location awareness suffers as a result.

climax.text: The best mobile breadcrumbs show only the parent: '← Phones' instead of the full path. One step back, always visible.

punchline.text: Show users where they are and how to go back. Breadcrumbs do both.

quiz.question: Why do breadcrumbs improve navigation?
options: They show location and let users jump to any parent level without using the back button
They make pages load faster by caching the path
They improve SEO rankings by adding internal links
